_banner: DO NOT MODIFY - frozen reference vectors. See CONTRIBUTING.md. Any change requires CODEOWNER review and a cited source.
_description: Pure LMS / modified-z formula checks against CDC's own published worked examples, using CDC's exact (rounded-for-publication) L, M, S rather than a value read from a data file. These validate the math in scripts/growth.py independently of table loading/interpolation.

vectors.citation: CDC, 'Modified z-scores in the CDC growth charts', https://www.cdc.gov/growth-chart-training/media/pdfs/Modified-Z-scores-508.pdf, p.2. 200-month-old girl, BMI 33 kg/m2, L=-2.18, M=20.76, S=0.148.
vectors.note: CDC's report states this rounds to 1.97 (97.6th percentile).

vectors.citation: Same source, p.2-3. Same girl, BMI mis-entered as 333 kg/m2 (a plausible fat-fingered '33.3').
vectors.note: CDC's report states this rounds to 3.1 -- illustrating that a 300 kg/m2 data-entry error only moves the ordinary z-score by ~1.1 SD, which is why the modified z-score exists (see modified_z vectors below).

vectors.citation: Same source, p.3. Same 333 kg/m2 example, using the modified-z formula (Equation in 'Calculation of modified z-scores').
vectors.note: CDC's report hand-computes this as 49.2 using SD_distance rounded to 6.32 kg/m2; our unrounded implementation of the same formula (SD_distance = 6.3182...) gives 49.42. The two are consistent given the report's stated rounding -- both correctly flag this as extreme (BIV cutoff for BMI-for-age is modified z > 8).

vectors.citation: Same source, p.3-4. Same sex/age, BMI 12 kg/m2 (below the median).
vectors.note: CDC's report states this rounds to -4.1, matching our unrounded value to 2 decimal places.
